output:
## Solution

**Yes.** If uvzi occurs, it is more likely that zuph occurred.

### Step-by-step

**Given:**
- P(zuph) = 0.77, so P(not zuph) = 0.23
- P(not zuph ∧ uvzi) = 0.11
- P(zuph ∧ uvzi) = 0.46

**Total probability of uvzi:**

P(uvzi) = P(zuph ∧ uvzi) + P(not zuph ∧ uvzi) = 0.46 + 0.11 = **0.57**

**Posterior via Bayes' rule:**

P(zuph | uvzi) = P(zuph ∧ uvzi) / P(uvzi) = 0.46 / 0.57 ≈ **0.8070** (80.7%)

P(not zuph | uvzi) = 0.11 / 0.57 ≈ **0.1930** (19.3%)

### Comparison

| | Probability |
|---|---|
| P(zuph \| uvzi) | **80.7%** |
| P(not zuph \| uvzi) | 19.3% |
| Prior P(zuph) | 77.0% |

Observing uvzi makes zuph more likely than not (80.7% vs 19.3%), and also slightly increases the probability of zuph relative to its prior (80.7% vs 77.0%). This makes sense because uvzi is more likely to occur when zuph is present — P(uvzi | zuph) ≈ 59.7% vs P(uvzi | not zuph) ≈ 47.8%.
